page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-lc1m2.small is an entry-level, burstable virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single vCPU mapped to one physical core and one thread, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports x86_64 architecture with instruction set extensions including AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

In terms of performance, the burstable CPU allocation model limits sustained high-compute workloads. Benchmark results place this instance in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both yield a score of 1042.98, reflecting its single-threaded nature. Memory bandwidth tests show 73.95 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 8.7 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 7.34 GB/sec for large uncached writes. PassMark metrics further confirm this profile, with a CPU score of 1239.52 and a memory score of 653.25.

The instance is designed for high cost efficiency in workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that experience occasional activity spikes. It achieves 1.12 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 0.86 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. Large language model inference on a small 135M model yields 53.79 tokens per second for prompt processing and 9.43 tokens per second for text generation.

Given these resource constraints and performance characteristics, the ecs.t5-lc1m2.small is best suited for lightweight, non-critical applications. Appropriate workloads include low-traffic web servers, microservices, development or testing environments, and basic task scheduling where continuous high CPU performance is not required.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-lc1m2.small is a burstable, entry-level virtual server powered by a single-core Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. Featuring 2.0 GB of RAM, x86_64 architecture, and no local storage, this instance utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model designed for low-baseline workloads. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with a stress-ng CPU score of 1042.98 and a PassMark CPU score of 1239.52. Memory bandwidth reaches 73.95 GB/sec for small cached blocks but drops significantly for larger blocks. This instance is highly cost-efficient for resource-light applications, delivering 1.12 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 0.86 GB/sec in static web serving. It is best suited for lightweight development environments, low-traffic web servers, and simple microservices.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-lc1m2.small is a burstable virtual server with 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8163 processor, designed for lightweight workloads and budget-conscious hosting.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-lc1m2.small is a burstable 1-vCPU virtual server with 2.0 GB RAM, ideal for low-traffic applications and testing.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable single-core virtual server designed for lightweight utility workloads and low-traffic development environments.

2.0 GB of system memory with a burstable CPU allocation model
No local storage and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers 1.12 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, and testing
